response.justification: Candidate is a strong full-stack/web engineer with broad polyglot experience, but the role requires 8 years of C++ as a hard minimum and significant large-scale distributed infrastructure leadership. Their C++ exposure is limited to a ~6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015, and core experience centers on TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web platforms rather than cloud compute platform architecture. No formal technical-leadership role mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers is evident.
strengths: Broad polyglot engineering background across web, mobile, embedded, and backend systems
Demonstrated mentoring/teaching via workshops, dojos, and Selenium course development
Experience with high-throughput micro-services (Dunnhumby/Sociomantic) handling millions of requests/sec
concerns: Falls drastically short of the 8-year C++ requirement (only ~6 months of C/C++ at NI)
No production cloud compute platform architecture or distributed infrastructure leadership experience
No formal Technical Lead title or evidence of leading cloud/DevOps engineering teams

key_factors.factor: Lack of large-scale cloud compute platform / distributed infra leadership
